channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Où et quand regarder le direct d’Artemis II qui survole la Lune ? YouTube, NASA+, Netflix, Prime Video et plus encore
item.description: Ce soir, la mission Artemis II passe au plus près de la surface lunaire. Entre les photos haute définition et les vidéos laser en 4K, la NASA a mis les petits plats dans les grands pour nous faire vivre l'événement. Voici les horaires précis pour la France métropolitaine.

item.title: Voici pourquoi il ne faut pas acheter l’Oppo Find N6
item.description: L'Oppo Find N6 est probablement le pliant le plus abouti qu'on ait eu entre les mains. Pliure quasi invisible, épaisseur ridicule, performances solides et photo Hasselblad convaincante. Seul problème : il ne sera pas commercialisé en France. Alors autant se persuader qu'on n'en a pas besoin.

item.title: Apple plans Supreme Court appeal in Epic Games App Store battle — again
item.description: Apple plans to ask the Supreme Court to review its App Store fight with Epic Games, as it challenges a ruling limiting its ability to charge fees on external payments.

item.title: I compared Artemis II mission's historic dark side of the moon photo with my Sony Alpha A6000, and the differences just blew me away
item.description: The Artemis II mission is starting to capture never-before-viewed (by human eyes) views of the moon, and this should excite stargazers and photographers.

item.title: Check Your Medicine Cabinet for These Eye Drop Bottles After Millions Recalled
item.description: The eye drops are sold at Rite-Aid, Walgreens, CVS, and more.

item.title: Artemis 2 Astronauts’ Epic iPhone Photos Are All… Selfies?
item.description: Turns out the incredible photos taken by the astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft are from the iPhone 17 Pro Max's front-facing camera and not its better rear shooters.

item.title: Pourquoi Google sort-il ses meilleures pépites IA sur iPhone ? Voici de la dictée vocale hors-ligne et gratuite
item.description: Google vient de sortir une application assez mystérieuse sur iOS : Google AI Edge Eloquent. Cette application promet de transformer vos notes vocales brouillonnes en textes impeccables, le tout sans abonnement et potentiellement sans connexion.

item.title: Il aspire tout, sauf vos économies : ce robot aspirateur-laveur Xiaomi avec sa station complète ne dépasse pas les 500 €
item.description: Dévoilé en 2025, le Xiaomi Robot Vacuum 5 est un aspirateur robot qui présente un très bon rapport qualité/prix. Actuellement, la marque le propose à 499,99 euros au lieu de 649,99 euros.

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II mission to fly around the far side of the Moon
item.description: Humans haven’t stepped foot on the Moon since NASA’s Apollo 17 mission in 1972. Now, the space agency is racing to get back to the lunar surface under the umbrella of its Artemis program — a nod to the Greek goddess and twin sister of Apollo, whose name was given to NASA’s first program to […]

item.title: OpenAI Releases Its Vague Vision for Reorganizing Society Around Superintelligence
item.description: The company behind ChatGPT announced a set of policy recommendations for the AI era including taxes on automated labor and a public wealth fund.

item.title: Stickers, live lobster, plush toys, and queues: No, it's not a rock concert, it's China's insatiable appetite for AI's new superstar, OpenClaw
item.description: OpenClaw mania sweeps China as AI automates tasks, excites tech enthusiasts, raises cybersecurity alarms, and sparks workforce anxiety.

item.title: 28-hour battery life, waterproof, and 5G: Acer's rugged device ventures where other routers fear to tread
item.description: Acer Connect M6E 5G provides rugged, all-day mobile Wi-Fi with 5G, NFC, Wi-Fi 6E, and global network support.

item.title: You can now search through app reviews on the Google Play Store
item.description: Finding the right app on the Google Play Store just got a lot easier. Google is rolling out a new search function for an app's reviews when navigating its Play Store, as first spotted by Android Authority. The new feature only currently searches for exact matches of at least two words, but it should still help users identify if certain apps and games have specific features or gameplay elements. You can find the search function after hitting the "See all reviews" button on the Play Store, and it will also offer some popular search terms next to the feature. It's similar to Amazon's "Search cust...

item.title: One of Our Favorite 360 Cams Is 35 Percent Off
item.description: You can score over $200 off the DJI Osmo 360.

item.title: Lego Is Getting Mark Hamill on the Phone to Sell You Its Smart Bricks
item.description: In an unprecedented move, the brick maker has tapped the 'Star Wars' star to sell you on its controversial smart play sets.

item.title: Netflix just released a standalone gaming app for kids
item.description: Netflix just released a free app called Playground for smartphones and tablets. This is a gaming app for kids, aged eight and under. It's available to all Netflix members on any tier, and the company promises it doesn't have ads or in-app purchases. It also works without a mobile or Wi-Fi connection. Netflix says this makes it the "perfect companion for long airplane rides or grocery trips." Kids do love their screens. The company promises an "ever-growing library of games" for children. The platform launches with titles based on some massively popular franchises. There's something called Play...

item.title: Watch this video of how a job interviewer exposes a North Korean fake IT worker
item.description: An apparent North Korean worker was caught visibly stumped during a remote job interview when asked to insult the country's leader.

item.title: Netflix is launching a new app for kids games
item.description: Netflix has made family-friendly titles a key part of its current games strategy, and now it's pushing a little harder with the launch of a new app. Called Netflix Playground, the app is focused on games aimed at kids aged eight and under, and, like the rest of Netflix's gaming lineup, Playground games are included […]

item.title: Gozney Dome Gen 2 Review: The Ultimate Backyard Flex
item.description: This colossal pizza oven is both pricey and impractical. It also makes the best pizza I’ve ever cooked at home.

item.title: North Korea’s hijack of one of the web’s most used open source projects was likely weeks in the making
item.description: North Korean hackers pushed out malicious updates to a popular open source project by hacking a top developer's computer in a long-running campaign.

item.title: Skip Target: Walmart's spring patio sale is better than I expected — here's everything I'm adding to my cart
item.description: I'm so impressed with Walmart's spring sale that I'm sharing everything I'm adding to my cart, including patio furniture, planters, grills, and more.

item.title: Scientists Are Recycling Centuries-Old Bullets for a Badly Needed Toxic Compound
item.description: Researchers want to recycle more lead waste for high-tech solar panels via a new method.

item.title: Hisense just launched the CanvasTV in a 50-inch size
item.description: Shop the new 50-inch Hisense CanvasTV at Amazon for $1,299.99.

item.title: Claude was down – here's everything we know
item.description: Claude identified an issue and rolled out a fix

item.title: OpenAI doesn’t expect to be profitable until at least 2030 as AI costs surge
item.description: New data on OpenAI and Anthropic shows a long road to profits. As OpenAI and Anthropic move closer to their planned initial public offerings, more details about the finances of both artificial intelligence giants are starting to emerge. It was no secret these companies were bleeding cash, but seeing the actual numbers is still striking.

item.title: Need a new smartwatch? The Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 is on sale and selling fast
item.description: As of April 6, you can get the Samsung Galaxy Watch 8 for $289.99 at Amazon, down from $349.99.

item.title: Going viral from space: NASAs tech-savvy Artemis II crew
item.description: NASA's Artemis II crew is going viral in space thanks to iPhone selfies, Instagram Reels, and a "Full House"-inspired video.

item.title: How to watch the Artemis II moon flyby live
item.description: It's Artemis II moon flyby day. Here's how to watch the flyby live and witness history being made.

item.title: SparkCat malware returns to target Android and iOS users, hiding in innocent apps to try and steal your details
item.description: The malware primarily targets crypto users in Japan, Korea, and China.

item.title: The Sony WH-1000XM5 headphones are $5 away from their lowest price ever
item.description: As of April 6, the Sony WH-1000XM6 headphones are on sale for $248 at Amazon, putting them just $5 away from their record-low price of $243, and slashing $151.99 off their price overall.

item.title: I revived an 1820s sea shanty with AI, and it’s a banger
item.description: Inspired by ShantyTok, I set out to make a modern earworm. My kids have been really into sea shanties lately (my family has eclectic musical tastes.)

item.title: Artemis II astronauts took these selfies of Earth with an iPhone 17
item.description: NASA's Artemis II crew brought iPhone 17s onboard to document their journey. See the first images they just sent back.

item.title: Samsung’s Galaxy S27 ‘Pro’ could squeeze in between the Ultra and Plus phones
item.description: Samsung's S27 lineup, expected next year, might include a new member, the Galaxy S27 Pro, which would be a second premium option between Samsung's Ultra and Plus models, as first reported by ETNews. The new Pro model reportedly won't support the S Pen but will feature Samsung's Privacy Display, which is currently only available on […]

item.title: You Can Buy ‘Dune: Part Three’ IMAX Tickets Right Now
item.description: 'Dune: Part Three' is really stepping out over 'Avengers: Doomsday' by selling select tickets eight months early.

item.title: What is the release date for Invincible season 4 episode 6 on Prime Video?
item.description: Here's when you'll be able to watch the sixth episode of Invincible's fourth season on Prime Video.

item.title: Battlefield 6 audio team says they 'constantly polish what we think needs polishing'
item.description: The Battlefield 6 audio team has discussed its work with the game's live service support.

item.title: How MassMutual and Mass General Brigham turned AI pilot sprawl into production results
item.description: Enterprise AI programs rarely fail because of bad ideas. More often, they get stuck in ungoverned pilot mode and never reach production. At a recent VentureBeat event, technology leaders from MassMutual and Mass General Brigham explained how they avoided that trap — and what the results look like when discipline replaces sprawl.At MassMutual, the results are concrete: 30% developer productivity gains, IT help desk resolution times reduced from 11 minutes to one, and customer service calls cut from 15 minutes to just one or two.“We're always starting with why do we care about this problem?” Sea...

item.title: The Boys has overstayed its welcome
item.description: "The Boys" fifth and final season is the show's darkest, most dour outing yet. Review.

item.title: J’ai testé le Leica Q3, le compact plein format qui vous force à voir le monde autrement
item.description: Le Leica Q3 est un compact plein format à objectif fixe. 60 mégapixels, un Summilux 28 mm f/1,7 stabilisé, un autofocus hybride, la vidéo 8K. À 6 250 euros. C'est beaucoup. C'est même une somme déraisonnable pour beaucoup de photographes. On fait le point sur ses qualités mais aussi ses défauts.

item.title: Shorter workweeks and cancer cures: Chase Bank boss Jamie Dimon puts an optimistic spin on AI disruption
item.description: In his annual shareholder letter, the CEO of JPMorgan Chase acknowledged that AI will cause job losses but seemed to argue the tech will be a net positive. JPMorgan Chase released its 2025 annual report today, including letters to shareholders from senior executives. In his letter, chairman and CEO Jamie Dimon shared his thoughts on artificial intelligence (AI) and the company’s plans to embrace it. ...

item.title: OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day work week
item.description: OpenAI proposes taxes on AI profits, public wealth funds, and expanded safety nets to address job loss and inequality, blending redistribution with capitalism as policymakers debate AI’s economic impact.

item.title: Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
item.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has published a video threatening OpenAI's planned Abu Dhabi datacenter if the US follows through on threats to attack the country's power plants, as reported earlier by Tom's Hardware. The video, which was published to an Iranian state-backed news outlet's X account on April 3rd, says the IRGC will […]...
